You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository.
The codebase is at version 10.0 (commit: 4a30419e).

## Issue to Resolve

Always mark read when archiving email messages
### Checklist

- [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same feature request.
- [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.
- [X] This issue only contains a request for one single feature, **not** multiple (related) features.

### App version

6.804

### Problem you are trying to solve

I'd love some way to mark things as read when archiving.

Generally, when archiving something unread, it's because I have sufficient information from the subject line/already roughly know the contents - or know I don't care too much about it, but still want it "just in case". I like being able to filter by unread though, especially if it's something I want to get back to later and refile - but don't want in my inbox because it's not really a priority. 

### Suggested solution

An option to mark things read when archiving, like what's done with spam, would be great. Alternatively I think it'd be similarly nice to have one of the possible swipe actions be "mark as read and archive" together.

### Screenshots / Drawings / Technical details

_No response_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Honestly I think this should be the default. I can't think of a use case to archive a message and not also mark it read.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `legacy/core/build.gradle.kts`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/ArchiveOperations.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/KoinModule.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/MessagingController.java`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue

- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

212→        var ident = 0
213→        var gotOne: Boolean
214→        do {
215→            gotOne = false
216→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", null)
217→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", null)
218→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$ident", false)

221→            val replyTo = storage.getString("$accountUuid.replyTo.$ident", null)
222→            if (email != null) {
223→                val identity = Identity(
224→                    name = name,
225→                    email = email,
226→                    signatureUse = signatureUse,
227→                    signature = signature,
228→                    description = description,
229→                    replyTo = replyTo,
230→                )
231→                newIdentities.add(identity)
232→                gotOne = true
233→            }
234→            ident++
235→        } while (gotOne)
236→
237→        if (newIdentities.isEmpty()) {
238→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.name", null)
239→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.email", null)

527→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
528→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
529→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
530→
531→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
532→            .apply {

534→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
535→            }
536→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
537→
538→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
539→    }

content.text:
The issue requested a feature to mark messages as read when archiving them, preferably as a default behavior. I have implemented this by adding a new configuration option `isMarkMessageAsReadOnArchive` to the `Account` class and updating the `ArchiveOperations` controller to respect this setting.

### Summary of Changes

1.  **Modified `legacy/account/src/main/java/app/k9mail/legacy/account/Account.kt`**:
    *   Added a new property `isMarkMessageAsReadOnArchive` which defaults to `true`. This ensures the requested behavior is the default.

2.  **Modified `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/AccountPreferenceSerializer.kt`**:
    *   Updated `loadAccount` to read the new setting from preferences.
    *   Updated `save` to persist the new setting.
    *   Updated `delete` to remove the setting when the account is deleted.
    *   Updated `loadDefaults` to set the default value to `true`.

3.  **Modified `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/ArchiveOperations.kt`**:
    *   Updated the `archiveMessages` (private) function to check `account.isMarkMessageAsReadOnArchive`.
    *   If enabled, it calls `messagingController.setFlag` to mark the messages as `Flag.SEEN` (read) before proceeding with the archive operation (move).
    *   This applies to both archiving individual messages/lists and archiving threads, as all paths converge to this method.

These changes ensure that when a user archives a message (or thread), it is automatically marked as read if the option is enabled (which it is by default), fulfilling the user request. The implementation uses existing `MessagingController` methods (`setFlag` and `moveOrCopyMessageSynchronous`) to perform the operations reliably.

### Verified Files
- `legacy/account/src/main/java/app/k9mail/legacy/account/Account.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/AccountPreferenceSerializer.kt`
- `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/controller/ArchiveOperations.kt`

526→    fun move(editor: StorageEditor, account: Account, storage: Storage, newPosition: Int) {
527→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
528→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
529→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
530→
531→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
532→            .apply {
533→                removeAt(oldPosition)
534→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
535→            }
536→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
537→
538→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
539→    }
